name: Denver Airport
description: The Denver International Airport (DIA) is the subject of numerous conspiracy theories that allege hidden meanings and nefarious purposes behind its art, architecture, and construction. Theories range from DIA being a secret headquarters for the New World Order (NWO) to housing underground bunkers for elites in the event of a global catastrophe. Proponents cite the airport's murals, which depict scenes of environmental destruction and global peace, its dedication plaque featuring Masonic symbols and a reference to the "New Airport Authority," and its unusual runway layout resembling a swastika (when viewed from above). The airport's construction delays and cost overruns have further fueled speculation. Mainstream explanations attribute the murals to the artist's (Leo Tanguma) intended themes of peace and environmentalism. The dedication plaque is explained as a standard practice for public works projects involving Masonic lodges. The runway layout is dismissed as a result of practical considerations for efficient aircraft operations, with any resemblance to a swastika being coincidental. Construction delays and cost overruns are attributed to the complexities of a large-scale infrastructure project and changing design requirements.
subtitle: Conspiracy theories surrounding Denver International Airport

claims.claim: DIA is a secret headquarters for the New World Order.
claims.evidence: Masonic symbols on dedication plaque, alleged underground bunkers, strategic location.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence of underground bunkers or NWO activity. Masonic symbols are common on public works projects.

claims.claim: The airport's murals depict a dystopian future and the coming apocalypse.
claims.evidence: Imagery of dead animals, environmental destruction, and global conflict in the murals.
claims.criticism: The artist's stated intent was to depict the horrors of war and pollution, and to promote themes of peace and environmentalism.

claims.claim: The runway layout resembles a swastika.
claims.evidence: Aerial views of the runways showing a pattern similar to a swastika.
claims.criticism: The runway layout is designed for efficient aircraft operations and any resemblance to a swastika is coincidental.

keyFigures.name: Leo Tanguma
keyFigures.role: Artist who created the controversial murals

keyFigures.name: Jesse Ventura
keyFigures.role: Former governor and conspiracy theorist who investigated the airport

keyFigures.name: Annie Jacobsen
keyFigures.role: Journalist who wrote about the airport's construction and potential uses

timeline.event: Denver International Airport opens after delays and cost overruns.
Conspiracy theories about the airport begin to circulate online.
Conspiracy theories gain traction through documentaries, books, and online forums.

sources.author: Annie Jacobsen
sources.work: Area 51: An Uncensored History of America's Top Secret Military Base
sources.title: Annie Jacobsen
